title: cassia obtusifolia var. humilis
intro: Cassia obtusifolia var. humilis, also known as Senna alexandrina, is a plant species that belongs to the pea family and is native to Africa, Asia, and the Middle East. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as treating digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating menstrual cramps, due to its high concentration of sennosides, which act as a natural laxative. Cassia obtusifolia var. humilis can be prepared into herbal teas, capsules, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
